about: The consumer's git-tracked, human-authored demand ledger — phrases the app needs that the GOLD catalog does not have yet. `scripts/register_demand.py` PUSHES these into the shared catalog's `requests` table (assets.db), where the curator reads them and replies via `fulfilled_by`. This JSON is the authored source; the DB row is the shared interop surface, written only by that tool (single writer per column → no drift). See docs/audio-clip-contract.md §8 and docs/handoff-demand-tables.md.
invariants: This file lists ONLY open requests. Presence here == an open row in assets.db `requests`.

Fulfillment is migration, not a flag. The producer sets `requests.fulfilled_by` on promotion; the consumer then wires the asset_id into clips/clips.manifest.json, adds a `usage` row, and DELETES the request from here (which deletes the DB row on the next register-demand). 'Done' == 'gone'.

In-use dependencies are NOT authored here. clips/clips.manifest.json is the authoritative list of asset_ids we ship; register_demand.py mirrors it into the `usage` table for the 'don't de-curate these' signal.
Run `make register-demand` to push this ledger into assets.db and read back fulfilled_by (OPEN / READY / STALE).

used_in: causality/7.1 — spider climbs UP the spout
requests.note: Completes the in/out/up/down concept vocabulary. 'out' is already gold (asset_d703e812fbff) in your new Words group; up/down/in are the natural siblings the causality and spatial scenes want.

used_in: causality/7.1 — water washes the spider DOWN
spatial/4.1 — put the ball IN the box
peekaboo — the animal is hiding IN
requests.note: Completes in/out. Lower priority than up/down since 'in the box' reads fine as a full TTS phrase today.
